canto_title: The Vision of the Celestial Image and the Prophecy of Nations

scenes.summary: Dante describes the beautiful, radiant Image that appears before him, capable of both sight and sound.
The Image speaks, explaining that it is elevated to glory due to its justice and piety, despite a worldly reputation of infamy.
Dante perceives the many individual lights of the Image as a single, unified warmth and sound.
Dante addresses the celestial flowers, asking them to satisfy his spiritual hunger and resolve his doubts.
The Image moves like a falcon, spreading its wings in a gesture of divine praise.
The Image explains how the fall of the first prideful creature limits the capacity of all lesser beings to grasp infinite goodness.
The Image describes how human vision, like an eye in the sea, can see the seabed but cannot perceive its true depth.
The Image addresses the dilemma of virtuous pagans who die without Christ, explaining that true light requires divine grace.
The Image questions Dante's right to judge the far reaches of the world with such limited perspective.
The Image condemns the coarse minds of humanity and explains that all good things flow from the first Divine Will.
The Image settles itself with the maternal grace of a stork tending to its young.
The Image issues a cryptic warning that its melodies are as incomprehensible to mortals as eternal judgment.
The lights of the Holy Spirit settle into the sign that once made the Romans venerable.
The Image declares that only those who believe in Christ enter this kingdom, and distinguishes the true Christian from the false.
The Image predicts the political and moral decline of various Western powers, including Prague, France, and Spain.

scenes.scene_name: Prophecies of the Mediterranean and Italy
scenes.summary: The vision foretells the corruption of Jerusalem, the greed of the islands, and the instability of the Italian territories.

scenes.scene_name: Prophecies of the North and East
scenes.summary: The prophecy concludes with the fates of Portugal, Norway, and Russia, and offers blessings for Hungary and Navarre.
